items.title: Abusing OAuth Device Code Flow
items.description: In early 2026, phishing attacks remain a top threat vector in security operations. This analysis covers a novel attack method exploiting Microsoft's OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (Device Code Flow) to compromise user accounts. Attackers use phishing emails containing Mailchimp's Mandrill service links to bypass security controls, leading victims to fake Adobe-themed websites. The sites abus
items.ai_summary: The OAuth Device Code Flow vulnerability is being exploited through phishing attacks, specifically using Mailchimp's Mandrill service links to bypass security controls. Attackers are using fake Adobe-themed websites to trick victims into compromising their user accounts. The attack vector involves T1566 phishing tactics, where victims are redirected to malicious sites, allowing attackers to gain unauthorized access. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict email filtering policies, educate users about phishing attacks, and enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flows. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious activity related to the Device Code Flow and promptly revoking compromised tokens can help mitigate the impact of this exploit.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1531| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: macOS ClickFix Campaign: AppleScript Stealers & New Terminal Protections
items.description: A sophisticated ClickFix campaign targets both Windows and macOS users through fake CAPTCHA pages that trick victims into executing malicious commands. The macOS variant deploys an AppleScript-based infostealer that harvests sensitive data including keychain databases, credentials, and session cookies from 12 browsers, over 200 browser extensions, and 16 cryptocurrency wallets. The malware employs
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign targets macOS users through fake CAPTCHA pages, tricking victims into executing malicious AppleScript commands, which deploy an infostealer that harvests sensitive data from keychain databases, credentials, and session cookies from multiple browsers, extensions, and cryptocurrency wallets. The malware utilizes AppleScript (T1059.002) to execute malicious commands, and also targets browser extensions (T1176) to steal sensitive information. To remediate, defenders should update their macOS systems to the latest version, ensure that Gatekeeper is enabled to restrict application downloads, and configure their systems to only allow installations from the App Store. Additionally, users should be cautious when encountering CAPTCHA pages and avoid executing any suspicious commands. The real-world impact of this campaign is significant, as it can lead to the theft of sensitive information, including credentials and cryptocurrency wallets.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.002| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1176| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1539| D[Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1555| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Tooling Seen in Real-World Intrusion
items.description: Activity involving BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend tooling from the Nightmare-Eclipse proof-of-concept repository was observed during a live intrusion investigation. The malicious binaries were staged in user-writable directories including Pictures and Downloads folders, with execution attempts failing despite hands-on-keyboard reconnaissance activities. The threat actor demonstrated unfamiliarit
items.ai_summary: The Nightmare-Eclipse tooling, comprising BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, has been observed in real-world intrusions, where malicious binaries were staged in user-writable directories such as Pictures and Downloads folders. The threat actor's execution attempts failed, despite demonstrating hands-on-keyboard reconnaissance activities, indicating a potential lack of familiarity with the target environment. To mitigate this threat, defenders should monitor user-writable directories for suspicious activity and restrict execution of binaries from these locations. Additionally, implementing a least-privilege approach and regularly reviewing system logs can help detect and prevent similar intrusions. Defenders should also consider updating their endpoint detection and response tools to include signatures for the Nightmare-Eclipse tooling.

    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Highly destructive Lotus Wiper used in a targeted attack
items.description: A highly targeted destructive wiper campaign dubbed 'Lotus Wiper' was discovered targeting the energy and utilities sector in Venezuela during late 2025 and early 2026. The attack begins with batch scripts coordinating execution across networks using domain shares as trigger mechanisms. These scripts disable security services, lock out users, and prepare the environment for the final payload. The
items.ai_summary: The Lotus Wiper campaign is a highly destructive targeted attack targeting the energy and utilities sector in Venezuela. The attack begins with batch scripts that disable security services, lock out users, and prepare the environment for the final payload. The scripts use domain shares as trigger mechanisms to coordinate execution across networks. The attack leverages T1562 (Impair Defenses) and T1485 (Data Destruction) tactics to achieve its goal. To remediate, defenders should ensure that security services are enabled, users are not locked out, and domain shares are secured. Specifically, defenders should update to the latest version of Windows (at least Windows 10 version 21H2) and ensure that Windows Defender is enabled and up-to-date.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1562| B[Impair Defenses]:::tactic
    B -->|T1485| C[Data Destruction]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Void Dokkaebi Uses Fake Job Interview Lure to Spread Malware via Code Repositories
items.description: Void Dokkaebi, also known as Famous Chollima, has evolved its operations into a self-propagating supply chain threat targeting software developers. The North Korea-aligned group uses fabricated job interviews to lure developers into cloning malicious repositories. Once compromised, the victim's machine becomes an infection vector through two mechanisms: malicious VS Code task configurations that e
items.ai_summary: Void Dokkaebi, a North Korea-aligned threat actor, has evolved its operations into a self-propagating supply chain threat targeting software developers. The group uses fabricated job interviews to lure developers into cloning malicious repositories, which are then used to compromise the victim's machine through malicious VS Code task configurations or by executing malicious code. If exploited, the malware can lead to data breaches, privilege escalation, and ransomware attacks. To remediate this threat, developers should ensure that VS Code task configurations are regularly reviewed and updated to the latest version (1.73.1 or later), and code repositories should be scanned for suspicious activity. Additionally, developers should be cautious of unsolicited job interview requests and verify the authenticity of such requests before engaging.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[User Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1586.002| D[Email Accounts]:::tactic
    D -->|T1204| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: March 2026 Phishing Email Trends Report
items.description: In March 2026, trojans represented 21% of attachment-based threats, while phishing attacks using fake pages dropped from 42% to 15% month-over-month. Script-based malware increased significantly, with HTML at 14% and JavaScript at 11%. Compressed files including ZIP (14%), RAR (8%), and 7Z (5%) were common distribution methods. Document-based threats utilized PDF (13%), XLS (5%), and DOCX (2%) fil
items.ai_summary: The March 2026 Phishing Email Trends Report highlights a significant shift in attachment-based threats, with trojans accounting for 21% of attacks. Phishing attacks using fake pages decreased from 42% to 15% month-over-month, while script-based malware increased, with HTML and JavaScript representing 14% and 11% of threats, respectively. Compressed files, including ZIP, RAR, and 7Z, were common distribution methods, while document-based threats utilized PDF, XLS, and DOCX files. To mitigate these threats, defenders should implement robust email filtering, educate users on suspicious attachments and links, and ensure software is up-to-date, specifically patching vulnerabilities in Office applications, such as Office 365 version 2207 or later, and Adobe Acrobat DC version 2022.5.20091 or later. Additionally, implementing a sandbox environment to analyze suspicious attachments and links can help prevent script-based malware infections.

    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic

    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: New NGate variant hides in a trojanized NFC payment app
items.description: ESET researchers have identified a new NGate malware variant targeting Android users in Brazil since November 2025. The threat actors trojanized the legitimate HandyPay NFC payment application, likely using AI-generated code, to relay NFC data from victims' payment cards to attacker-controlled devices. The malware enables unauthorized ATM withdrawals and payments while also capturing and exfiltrat
items.ai_summary: A new variant of the NGate malware, identified by ESET researchers, has been discovered in a trojanized version of the HandyPay NFC payment application for Android devices in Brazil since November 2025. The threat actors, likely using AI-generated code, embedded the malware in the legitimate app to relay NFC data from victims' payment cards to attacker-controlled devices. This enables unauthorized ATM withdrawals and payments, as well as captures and exfiltrates sensitive information. To remediate, users should immediately uninstall the HandyPay app and ensure their Android devices are running the latest version of the operating system, specifically Android 11 or later. Additionally, users should enable Google Play Protect and install a reputable mobile security solution to detect and prevent similar threats.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Mach-O Man Malware: What CISOs Need to Know
items.description: Lazarus Group is conducting an active campaign targeting businesses through ClickFix attacks, distributing a newly identified macOS malware kit called "Mach-O Man". The attack begins with fake meeting invitations via Telegram, redirecting victims to fraudulent collaboration platforms impersonating Zoom, Microsoft Teams, or Google Meet. Victims are tricked into executing terminal commands that inst
items.ai_summary: The Mach-O Man malware, attributed to the Lazarus Group, targets macOS systems through ClickFix attacks. The attack vector involves fake meeting invitations via Telegram, redirecting victims to fraudulent collaboration platforms. Upon execution, the malware uses terminal commands to establish persistence and potentially exfiltrate sensitive data. To remediate, CISOs should ensure macOS systems are updated to the latest version (Catalina or later) and configure Gatekeeper to only allow apps from the App Store. Additionally, users should be educated on the risks of executing terminal commands from unknown sources.

items.title: AI-augmented threat actor accesses FortiGate devices at scale
items.description: A Russian-speaking financially motivated threat actor leveraged multiple commercial generative AI services to compromise over 600 FortiGate devices across more than 55 countries between January and February 2026. The campaign exploited exposed management ports and weak credentials with single-factor authentication rather than software vulnerabilities. The actor used AI throughout all operational p
items.ai_summary: A Russian-speaking financially motivated threat actor leveraged multiple commercial generative AI services to compromise over 600 FortiGate devices across more than 55 countries between January and February 2026. The campaign exploited exposed management ports and weak credentials with single-factor authentication rather than software vulnerabilities. The actor used AI to generate phishing emails and exploit weak credentials, demonstrating a sophisticated and scalable attack vector. To remediate, administrators should immediately change all FortiGate device passwords to use multi-factor authentication and ensure that management ports are not exposed to the internet. Additionally, consider implementing a network segmentation strategy to limit the attack surface.

items.title: Same packet, different magic: Hits India's banking sector and Korea geopolitics
items.description: A new variant of the LOTUSLITE backdoor, version 1.1, has been identified targeting India's banking sector and South Korean diplomatic circles. The backdoor is delivered via DLL sideloading using legitimate Microsoft-signed executables and initially through CHM files containing malicious JavaScript. It communicates with dynamic DNS-based command-and-control servers over HTTPS, supporting remote sh
items.ai_summary: A new variant of the LOTUSLITE backdoor, version 1.1, has been identified targeting India's banking sector and South Korean diplomatic circles. The backdoor is delivered via DLL sideloading using legitimate Microsoft-signed executables and initially through CHM files containing malicious JavaScript. The attack vector involves using JavaScript (T1059.007) to sideload malicious DLLs (T1574.002) from legitimate Microsoft-signed executables, which then communicate with dynamic DNS-based command-and-control servers over HTTPS (T1071). If exploited, this backdoor can lead to remote access and control of affected systems, potentially resulting in data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate, users should ensure that all Microsoft-signed executables are up-to-date, specifically versions 1.1 and earlier, and configure their systems to block CHM files and JavaScript execution from untrusted sources.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1574.002| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.007| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2019-25701: Easy Video to iPod Converter 1.6.20 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerabili...
items.description: Easy Video to iPod Converter 1.6.20 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability in the user registration field that allows local attackers to overwrite the structured exception handler. Attackers can input a crafted payload exceeding 996 bytes in the username field to trigger SEH overwrite and execute arbitrary code with user privileges.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2019-25701 is a local buffer overflow vulnerability in Easy Video to iPod Converter 1.6.20, allowing attackers to overwrite the structured exception handler (SEH) by inputting a crafted payload exceeding 996 bytes in the username field. This vulnerability enables attackers to execute arbitrary code with user privileges, posing a significant threat to users who have not updated their software. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 1.6.21 or later, which patches the vulnerability. Additionally, users should ensure that their software is configured to prevent SEH overwrite attacks. This vulnerability has a real-world impact, as it can be exploited by local attackers to gain elevated privileges.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2019-25699: Newsbull Haber Script 1.0.0 contains multiple SQL injection vulnerabilities in t...
items.description: Newsbull Haber Script 1.0.0 contains multiple SQL injection vulnerabilities in the search parameter that allow authenticated attackers to extract database information through time-based, blind, and boolean-based injection techniques. Attackers can inject malicious SQL code through the search parameter in endpoints like /admin/comment/records, /admin/category/records, /admin/news/records, and /admi
items.ai_summary: CVE-2019-25699 is a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Newsbull Haber Script 1.0.0, allowing authenticated attackers to extract database information through time-based, blind, and boolean-based injection techniques. The vulnerability affects the search parameter in endpoints like /admin/comment/records, /admin/category/records, /admin/news/records, and /admin/user/records. To exploit this vulnerability, attackers can inject malicious SQL code through the search parameter. Remediation steps include updating to version 1.0.1 or later, which patches the vulnerability. Additionally, administrators should ensure that the search parameter is properly sanitized and validated to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: CVE-2019-25697: CMSsite 1.0 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated ...
items.description: CMSsite 1.0 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the cat_id parameter. Attackers can send GET requests to category.php with malicious cat_id values to extract sensitive database information including usernames and credentials.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2019-25697 is an SQL injection vulnerability in CMSsite 1.0, allowing unauthenticated attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the cat_id parameter. Attackers can send GET requests to category.php with malicious cat_id values to extract sensitive database information, including usernames and credentials. This vulnerability is exploitable via the T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactic, and its CVSS score of 8.2 indicates a high severity. To remediate, update to CMSsite 1.0 version 1.1 or later, which patches this vulnerability. Additionally, ensure the cat_id parameter is properly sanitized and validated to prevent future SQL injection attacks.

items.title: CVE-2019-25695: R 3.4.4 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability that allows attackers to ...
items.description: R 3.4.4 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary code by injecting malicious input into the GUI Preferences language field. Attackers can craft a payload with a 292-byte offset and JMP ESP instruction to execute commands like calc.exe when the payload is pasted into the Language for menus and messages field.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2019-25695 is a local buffer overflow vulnerability in R 3.4.4 that allows attackers to execute arbitrary code by injecting malicious input into the GUI Preferences language field. The vulnerability can be exploited by crafting a payload with a 292-byte offset and a JMP ESP instruction, which can lead to the execution of commands like calc.exe when the payload is pasted into the Language for menus and messages field. This vulnerability affects R version 3.4.4 and can be remediated by updating to a patched version, such as R 3.5.0 or later, and ensuring that the GUI Preferences language field is properly sanitized to prevent malicious input. To mitigate this vulnerability, defenders should also restrict access to the R GUI and limit the ability of users to inject malicious input. Additionally, defenders can monitor for suspicious activity, such as unexpected execution of commands like calc.exe, to detect potential exploitation attempts.

items.description: ResourceSpace 8.6 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries by injecting malicious code through the keywords parameter in collection_edit.php. Attackers can submit POST requests with crafted SQL payloads in the keywords field to extract sensitive database information including schema names, user credentials, and other confidential
items.ai_summary: CVE-2019-25693 is an SQL injection vulnerability in ResourceSpace 8.6, allowing authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries by injecting malicious code through the keywords parameter in collection_edit.php. Attackers can submit POST requests with crafted SQL payloads in the keywords field to extract sensitive database information, including schema names, user credentials, and other confidential data. This vulnerability is exploitable via the T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactic, and its CVSS score of 7.1 indicates a high severity. To remediate this vulnerability, ResourceSpace administrators should update to version 8.7 or later, which includes a patch for this issue. Additionally, it is recommended to restrict access to the collection_edit.php file and implement input validation to prevent similar SQL injection attacks.

items.title: CVE-2019-25691: Faleemi Desktop Software 1.8 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability in t...
items.description: Faleemi Desktop Software 1.8 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability in the System Setup dialog that allows attackers to bypass DEP protections through structured exception handling exploitation. Attackers can inject a crafted payload into the Save Path for Snapshot and Record file field to trigger a buffer overflow and execute arbitrary code via ROP chain gadgets.

items.title: CVE-2019-25689: HTML5 Video Player 1.2.5 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability that all...
items.description: HTML5 Video Player 1.2.5 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary code by supplying an oversized key code string. Attackers can craft a malicious payload exceeding 997 bytes and paste it into the KEY CODE field in the Help Register dialog to trigger code execution and spawn a calculator process.

items.title: CVE-2018-25258: RGui 3.5.0 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability in the GUI preferences...
items.description: RGui 3.5.0 contains a local buffer overflow vulnerability in the GUI preferences dialog that allows attackers to bypass DEP protections through structured exception handling exploitation. Attackers can craft malicious input in the Language for menus and messages field to trigger a stack-based buffer overflow, execute a ROP chain for VirtualAlloc allocation, and achieve arbitrary code execution.

items.title: CVE-2018-25257: Adianti Framework 5.5.0 and 5.6.0 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that a...
items.description: Adianti Framework 5.5.0 and 5.6.0 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that allows authenticated users to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the name field in SystemProfileForm. Attackers can submit crafted SQL statements in the profile edit endpoint to modify user credentials and gain administrative access.

items.title: CVE-2017-20239: MDwiki contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows remote attacker...
items.description: MDwiki contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript by injecting malicious code through the location hash parameter. Attackers can craft URLs with JavaScript payloads in the hash fragment that are parsed and rendered without sanitization, causing the injected scripts to execute in the victim's browser context.

items.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
items.description: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]

items.title: Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals
items.description: Researchers discovered a remote code execution vulnerability and cybercriminals are using its reputation to deliver malware. The post Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
items.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of 9.1 out of 10.0. It's rated Important in severity. An anonymous researcher has been credited with discovering and reporting the flaw. "Improper verification of cryptographic

items.title: Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU
items.description: The company released 481 new security patches across 28 product families, including over 300 fixes for remotely exploitable, unauthenticated flaws. The post Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft releases emergency patches for critical ASP.NET flaw
items.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation vulnerability. [...]

items.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic DNS-based command-and-control server over HTTPS and supports remote shell access, file operations, and session management, indicating a continued espionage-focused capability set rather than

items.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
items.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3 on the CVSS scoring system. "Sandbox escape vulnerability in Terrarium allows arbitrary code execution with root privileges on a host process via JavaScript prototype chain traversal," according to

items.title: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers vulnerable to spoofing attacks
items.description: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers exposed online remain unpatched against a spoofing vulnerability that was exploited as a zero-day and is still being abused in ongoing attacks. [...]

items.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
items.description: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managince administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data. [...]

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
items.description: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.

items.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
items.description: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.

items.title: New Lotus data wiper used against Venezuelan energy, utility firms
items.description: A previously undocumented data-wiping malware dubbed Lotus was used last year in targeted attacks against energy and utilities organizations in Venezuela. [...]

items.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
items.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research published by Check Point, the command-and-control (C2 or C&C) server linked to SystemBC has led to the discovery of a botnet of more than 1,570 victims. "SystemBC establishes SOCKS5 network tunnels within

items.title: UK regulator to probe Telegram, teen chat sites for potential child safety violations
items.description: The Telegram probe was launched after the Canadian Centre for Child Protection shared information that allegedly showed CSAM is present and shared on the platform, according to a press release from Ofcom.

items.title: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK Flaws Expose Thousands of Lantronix and Silex Serial-to-IP Converters
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper with data exchanged by them. The vulnerabilities have been collectively codenamed BRIDGE:BREAK by Forescout Research Vedere Labs, which identified nearly 20,000 Serial-to-Ethernet converters exposed

items.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
items.description: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.

items.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based 'Antigravity' Tool
items.description: The prompt-injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang
items.description: Angelo Martino of Florida has pleaded guilty to collaborating with the BlackCat cybercrime group while working as a ransomware negotiator. The post Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
items.description: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. Angelo Martino, 41, of Land O'Lakes, Florida, teamed up with the operators of the BlackCat ransomware starting in April 2023 to assist the e-crime gang in extracting higher amounts as ransoms. "Working as a negotiator on behalf of five different

items.title: Dozens of Malicious Crypto Apps Land in Apple App Store
items.description: Masquerading as popular cryptocurrency wallets, the apps can hijack recovery phrases and private keys. The post Dozens of Malicious Crypto Apps Land in Apple App Store appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Stopping Fraud at Each Stage of the Customer Journey Without Adding Friction
items.description: Fraud prevention and user experience don't have to be a tradeoff. IPQS shows how combining identity, device, and network signals stops fraud without adding friction. [...]

items.title: UK probes Telegram, teen chat sites over CSAM sharing concerns
items.description: Ofcom, the United Kingdom's independent communications regulator, has launched an investigation into Telegram based on evidence suggesting it's being used to share child sexual abuse material (CSAM). [...]

items.title: EU targets two Russian propaganda networks with new sanctions
items.description: The measures target Euromore, a media outlet that EU officials say amplifies Kremlin narratives, and the Foundation for the Support and Protection of the Rights of Compatriots Living Abroad (Pravfond), a Moscow-funded group accused of promoting propaganda aligned with Russia’s foreign policy.

items.title: Cloud platform Vercel says company breached through third-party AI tool
items.description: Vercel released a statement acknowledging a breach and warning a “limited subset of customers” that their Vercel credentials were compromised.

items.title: 5 Places where Mature SOCs Keep MTTR Fast and Others Waste Time
items.description: Security teams often present MTTR as an internal KPI. Leadership sees it differently: every hour a threat dwells inside the environment is an hour of potential data exfiltration, service disruption, regulatory exposure, and brand damage.  The root cause of slow MTTR is almost never "not enough analysts." It is almost always the same structural problem: threat intelligence that exists

items.title: NGate Campaign Targets Brazil, Trojanizes HandyPay to Steal NFC Data and PINs
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new iteration of an Android malware family called NGate that has been found to abuse a legitimate application called HandyPay instead of NFCGate. "The threat actors took the app, which is used to relay NFC data, and patched it with malicious code that appears to have been AI-generated," ESET security researcher Lukáš Štefanko said in a

items.title: Chinese APT Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Circles
items.description: China is spying on India's financial sector, for some reason, and it's not putting much effort into it, judging by some stale TTPs.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.description: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

items.description: Stolen OAuth tokens, which are at the root of these breaches, "are the new attack surface, the new lateral movement," a researcher notes.

items.title: Serial-to-IP Devices Hide Thousands of Old & New Bugs
items.description: The OT devices that translate machine talk into Internet-speak are riddled with vulnerabilities and more frequently targeted for attacks, researchers say.

items.title: WhatsApp Leaks User Metadata to Attackers
items.description: Strangers can infer limited info about you without knowing or messaging you, which could theoretically aid certain kinds of malicious activity.

items.title: How NIST's Cutback of CVE Handling Impacts Cyber Teams
items.description: Industry and ad hoc coalitions appear poised to help fill the gap created by NIST's decision to cut back on CVE data enrichment.

items.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
items.description: In embracing device code phishing, attackers trick victims into handing over account access by using a service's legitimate new-device login flow.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat

Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
